aiExplainer.label: Background
aiExplainer.title: How does an AI tool actually work?
aiExplainer.p1: When you ask ChatGPT or Google AI a question, you don't get a list of links – you get an answer. But that answer comes from somewhere: AI tools are trained on a massive mix of sources – Wikipedia, news sites, scientific articles, forums, and regular websites like yours.
aiExplainer.p2: Most AI tools learned the internet up to a certain date – they don't continuously crawl in real-time. But that doesn't mean your website doesn't matter. Tools like Google AI Overviews and Perplexity do actively crawl current content.
aiExplainer.p3: CrawlWizard shows you what a bot sees when it visits your website. Are your headings correct? Can a machine understand your content? Do you have the right structured data? We make it transparent.
aiExplainer.quote: Good structure is the language that search engines and AI speak. CrawlWizard helps you make this more transparent.

serverAi.title: Server AI
serverAi.description: For heavy tasks like FAQ generation, article writing, and AI Ranking Checks, CrawlWizard uses powerful large language models (LLMs) on our servers – think OpenAI and Google Gemini models. Your page content is sent as context, after which the model generates optimized output. This uses AI credits; the amount depends on your plan.

browserAi.title: Browser AI
browserAi.description: For quick relevance analyses, CrawlWizard runs a compact embedding model (MiniLM) directly in your browser via WebGPU. The model calculates the semantic similarity between an FAQ question and your page content using cosine similarity. No data leaves your device, no credits needed, and it works unlimited.

cta.title: Will your website be the source that gets cited?
cta.subtitle: Analyze your website for free and discover what can be improved for Google and AI tools.

keyword.title: Keyword Analysis
keyword.subtitle: Analysis of keyword density and relevance relative to the content
keyword.densityExplanation: Percentage of total word count. Optimal is between 1-3%.
keyword.relevanceExplanation: How well the keyword fits the context in which it appears.
keyword.stuffingExplanation: Too high density (>3%) can be seen as spam by search engines.

keywordPlacement.title: Keyword Placement Advice
keywordPlacement.checkPosition: Check if "{{keyword}}" is in the right positions
keywordPlacement.urlDescription: The main keyword appears in the URL
keywordPlacement.urlPass: The keyword is included in the URL. This helps search engines understand what the page is about.
keywordPlacement.urlFail: Consider adjusting the URL to include "{{keyword}}". A keyword-rich URL strengthens relevance for search engines.
keywordPlacement.h1Description: The main keyword appears in the H1 heading
keywordPlacement.h1Pass: The keyword is included in the H1. This is crucial for SEO because the H1 is the most important heading on the page.
keywordPlacement.h1Fail: Add "{{keyword}}" to the H1 heading. This is one of the most important on-page SEO factors.
keywordPlacement.introDescription: The main keyword appears in the first paragraph below the H1
keywordPlacement.introPass: The keyword is in the intro text. This directly strengthens context for search engines and users.
keywordPlacement.introFail: Include "{{keyword}}" in the first paragraph below the H1. This gives search engines immediate context about the topic.
keywordPlacement.urlImportance: A keyword in the URL is a strong relevance signal for search engines and improves click-through rate in search results.
keywordPlacement.h1Importance: The H1 is the most important heading and tells search engines directly what the page is about. There should be only one H1 per page.
keywordPlacement.introImportance: The first paragraph is given extra weight by search engines. Placing the keyword here strengthens topical relevance.

structuredData.title: Rich Snippets & Structured Data
structuredData.whyNotAllSchemas: Why don't I see all schemas?
structuredData.explanation: Not every page needs the same structured data. CrawlWizard analyzes the page type (homepage, product page, blog, etc.) and only shows relevant schemas. This prevents unnecessary or duplicate markup — something search engines can see as a negative signal.
structuredData.found: Structured data found on this page!
structuredData.notFound: No structured data found. This is a missed SEO opportunity!
structuredData.recommendedTypes: Recommended structured data types to add:
structuredData.tip1Desc: Add JSON-LD scripts in the <head> or <body> of your page. This is the most flexible and maintainable method.
structuredData.tip2Desc: Use Google's Rich Results Test tool to validate your structured data before going live.
structuredData.tip3Desc:
• Better visibility in search results
• Rich snippets with stars, prices, images
• Higher click-through rates (CTR)
• Better understanding of your content by search engines
structuredData.perfectScore: Excellent! This page has all basic structured data implemented.

aiRanking.title: AI Ranking Check
aiRanking.lockedDescription: Check if your website is mentioned by the most important AI models. Discover your position, mention, and sentiment per model.
aiRanking.checkDescription: Check if <1>{{domain}}</1> is mentioned by AI models
aiRanking.running: Querying AI models... This may take a moment
aiRanking.whichModelsDesc: Each question is submitted in parallel to 4 AI models: Gemini Pro, Gemini Flash, GPT-5 and GPT-5 Mini.
aiRanking.whatIsCheckedDesc: Per model we analyze if your domain is mentioned, at what position it appears, and what the sentiment is (positive, neutral, or negative).
aiRanking.whyMaxQuestionsDesc: Each question generates 4 AI requests (one per model). To keep credit usage manageable, the maximum is 5 questions per check (= 20 AI calls).

article.title: Content Article Generator
article.description: Generate an in-depth content article (1200-1800 words) based on this FAQ. The article is automatically checked for AI detection.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.cookieDescription: Below you can view and adjust your cookie preferences. Changes are applied immediately.
privacy.necessaryDesc: These cookies are essential for the website to function. They provide basic features such as page navigation, session management, and access to secure areas. Without these cookies, the website cannot function properly.
privacy.functionalDesc: Functional cookies remember your preferences and choices, such as your chosen theme (dark/light mode) and language settings. This improves your experience on repeat visits.
privacy.analyticsDesc: These cookies help us understand how visitors use the website by collecting anonymized information. This allows us to continuously improve our website.
privacy.marketingDesc: Marketing cookies are used to track visitors across websites. The goal is to show ads that are relevant and engaging for the individual user.
privacy.intro: CrawlWizard respects your privacy and ensures that your personal data is protected. This privacy policy applies to the website and services of CrawlWizard and explains what data we collect, why we do so, and how we protect it.
privacy.gdprCompliance: This policy complies with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and applies to all visitors and users of our service.
